g.text: MENU
BACK
What is a tesseract?
What style of music is this? What's the right attitude to play it?
Why did you make nested notation? What inspired its creation,
and who inﬂuenced its development?
Is there any kind of admin managing each session?
Is there an end to the piece?

g.serif:id: What's the best gear to bring
g.text: What's the best gear to bring to a nested notation session?
There's a wide variety of acceptable gear to bring to a nested notation session.
In terms of
musical instruments
, any self-contained portable rig is encouraged. This might mean
a guitar, pedalboard and small ampliﬁer, or a laptop and either hardware or software eﬀects, a
vocal mic and small ampliﬁer, or an unampliﬁed acoustic instrument such as a violin, trombone,
hand percussion, or voice.
AC power from an outlet is not guaranteed, but with luck and conversation, hopefully anyone
who needs it will ﬁnd it.
In terms of
digital hardware
, a suitable rig might be simply a pocket smartphone with no
speaker except its own, or a setup more suited to a musician's experience: a tablet computer
with a larger screen, like an iPad, Android tablet, or even a laptop, a tablet stand or music
stand which enables hands-free positioning of the device, and a bluetooth speaker or wired
ampliﬁer which makes a loud enough contribution to the acoustic environment.
A maxed-out 20W bluetooth speaker should be loud enough to be heard well in a nested
notation session, although a 60W speaker will sound better at a lower volume. A larger speaker
in a traditional enclosure will sound better still, although it will require AC power and sum the
stereo sources to mono.
If possible, it's recommended to bring:
An iPad or Android tablet with screen 10" or larger, a stand for this tablet, and a bluetooth
speaker with at least 50W of sound energy available.
